input_text: Max Verstappen dominated the 2023 Formula 1 season. He won the World Drivers' Championship and Red Bull won the Constructors' Championship. Sergio Pérez also finished second in the Drivers' Championship, giving Red Bull a one-two result in the standings.

expected_claims.claim: Max Verstappen won the 2023 Drivers' Championship.
Red Bull won the 2023 Constructors' Championship.
Sergio Pérez finished second in the 2023 Drivers' Championship.

notes: Good baseline for multi-claim extraction from a short article.

input_text: The 2021 Formula 1 season ended with Lewis Hamilton winning his eighth world title. Max Verstappen finished second after losing the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix. Mercedes still won the Constructors' Championship that year.

expected_claims.claim: Lewis Hamilton won the 2021 Drivers' Championship.
Max Verstappen finished second in the 2021 Drivers' Championship.
Mercedes won the 2021 Constructors' Championship.

notes: Contains false driver champion claim and true constructor champion claim.

input_text: Ferrari had a difficult 2023 Formula 1 season and finished third in the Constructors' Championship. Charles Leclerc and Carlos Sainz drove for the team. Some reports also claimed that Ferrari was considering replacing one of its drivers because of internal tension.

expected_claims.claim: Ferrari finished third in the 2023 Constructors' Championship.
Charles Leclerc and Carlos Sainz drove for Ferrari in 2023.
Ferrari was considering replacing one driver because of internal tension.

notes: First two claims are structured. Last claim is news/rumor and should use web.

input_text: The 2022 Bahrain Grand Prix was won by Charles Leclerc for Ferrari. Carlos Sainz finished second, also for Ferrari. Max Verstappen completed the podium in third place.

expected_claims.claim: Charles Leclerc won the 2022 Bahrain Grand Prix.
Carlos Sainz finished second in the 2022 Bahrain Grand Prix.
Max Verstappen finished third in the 2022 Bahrain Grand Prix.

notes: First two claims are true. Verstappen did not finish third in that race.

input_text: NVIDIA released several Jetson developer kits for edge AI workloads. These devices are often used for computer vision, robotics, OCR, and local inference. Developers can deploy Docker containers on Jetson devices to simplify production environments.

input_text: Toyota won the 24 Hours of Le Mans after a strong endurance racing performance. The race featured prototype cars, multi-class traffic, and long stints through the night.

input_text: Michael Schumacher and Lewis Hamilton are tied on seven Formula 1 World Drivers' Championships. Sebastian Vettel has four titles, while Fernando Alonso has two. Max Verstappen had already won three titles by the end of the 2023 season.

expected_claims.claim: Schumacher and Hamilton both have seven titles.
Vettel has four titles.
Alonso has two titles.
Verstappen had three titles by the end of 2023.

notes: Good test for multiple driver title counts.

input_text: A recent paddock rumor suggested that a top Formula 1 team is facing internal disagreement over its future driver lineup. The report claimed that senior figures are split between keeping the current drivers and signing a younger prospect. No official team statement has confirmed the story.

expected_claims.claim: A top F1 team is facing internal disagreement over its future driver lineup.
Senior figures are split between keeping current drivers and signing a younger prospect.
No official team statement has confirmed the story.

notes: Useful for testing rumor handling, weak sourcing, and cautious final explanation.

input_text: Max Verstappen won the 2023 Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship. In the same year, Manchester City won the UEFA Champions League. Red Bull also won the 2023 Formula 1 Constructors' Championship.

Red Bull won the 2023 F1 Constructors' Championship.

ignored_claims.claim: Manchester City won the UEFA Champions League in 2023.

notes: System should extract only F1-relevant claims or mark non-F1 claim as ignored.

input_text: The 2023 Formula 1 season was won by Max Verstappen, but the article also claims that Lewis Hamilton was officially listed as the 2023 champion. Red Bull won the Constructors' Championship that season.
